content: We can reframe natural biological pathogens (biological agents from Topic B) as naturally evolved intrinsic curiosity modules that drive autonomous exploration of host ecosystem niches, much like 2025's ICM-powered autonomous UAV swarm agents that seek out novel unknown targets to gain intrinsic reward. This analogy opens a new framework to engineer more robust ICM mechanisms by mimicking how biological agents evolve to prioritize novel, uncolonized host environments over familiar explored ones.
